response:
*KEEP THE AREA CLEAN*  
BRUSH GENTLY AND THOROUGHLY TWICE A DAY  
RINSE WITH WATER AFTER EVERY MEAL  
*FLOSS CAREFULLY*  
FLOSS AROUND THE AFFECTED TOOTH  
REMOVE FOOD PARTICLES GENTLY  
*AVOID CERTAIN FOODS*  
STAY AWAY FROM HARD OR STICKY FOODS  
AVOID EXTREME HOT OR COLD ITEMS  
*TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS*  
USE OVER THE COUNTERTOP FILLING  
PROTECT THE TOOTH UNTIL A DOCTOR COMES  
*MONITOR FOR CHANGES*  
KEEP AN EYE ON THE TOOTH FOR SIGNS OF INFECTION  
INCREASED PAIN OR SENSITIVITY MAY INDICATE A PROBLEM  

* * *  
*USE SALINE RINSE*  
RINSE WITH SALT WATER TO REDUCE INFLAMMATION  
BACTERIA IN THE MOUTH CAN BE PREVENTED  
*SEE DENTAL CARE IMMEDIATELY*  
LOOK FOR DENTAL SERVICES IN YOUR CURRENT LOCATION  
DENTAL CARE IS CRUCIAL TO PREVENT COMPLICATIONS  
*TRADE ONE FOR THE OTHER*  
CHOOSE A DENTAL SOLUTION THAT FITS YOUR NEEDS  
TEMPORARY SOLUTIONS CAN BE A GOOD START  
*BOX THE TOOTH*  
PROTECT THE TOOTH WITH A TEMPORARY FILLING  
SAFEGUARD AGAINST FURTHER DAMAGE  
*TWICE A DAY*  
BRUSH AND RINSE TO KEEP THE TOOTH CLEAN  
PROTECT THE TOOTH UNTIL A DOCTOR CAN TREAT IT
